query:
You are a SaaS pricing analyst. For each of the 10 companies below, extract their CURRENT pricing structure from their public pricing pages ({domain}/pricing, {domain}/plans), G2, Capterra, and reverse-engineering posts.

Companies:
  1. name="Coval", website="https://coval.ai"
  2. name="SiliconFlow", website="https://siliconflow.com"
  3. name="Together AI", website="https://together.ai"
  4. name="Workato", website="https://orkato.com"
  5. name="Northflank", website="https://northflank.com"
  6. name="Botpress", website="https://botpress.com"
  7. name="Hugging Face", website="https://huggingface.co"
  8. name="Altair AI Studio", website="https://altair.com"
  9. name="Fermyon", website="https://fermyon.com"
  10. name="AWS Bedrock", website="https://aws.amazon.com"

For EACH company extract:
- pricing_model_kind: one of Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, Hybrid
- free_plan: true/false
- starts_at_usd: lowest paid tier monthly USD (0 if free plan only, null if Enterprise-only / contact sales)
- mention: short UI tagline e.g. 'Starts at $20/user/mo' or 'Contact sales' or 'Free + paid plans from $8/mo'
- tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, features (3-6 strings), target}. Include ALL public tiers (e.g. Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise). For Enterprise tiers without public price, set price_monthly_usd=null and target='Enterprise'.
- recent_changes: any pricing change in last 12 months (date + source). Leave blank if none.

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent prices. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
answer:
{"executive_summary": "- **Usage-Based Dominance**: 4 of 10 companies (SiliconFlow, Together AI, AWS Bedrock, Northflank) use primarily usage-based or hybrid pricing with per-token/per-vCPU billing -> buyers must model variable spend carefully before committing.
- **Freemium Entry Points**: 7 of 10 companies offer a free plan or free tier, lowering adoption barriers -> evaluate free-tier limits against production needs before relying on them.
- **Enterprise Opacity**: Workato and Altair AI Studio publish zero public prices; all tiers require sales quotes -> budget forecasting requires vendor engagement, creating negotiation leverage but also procurement friction.
- **Hybrid Pricing Emergence**: Botpress and Hugging Face combine subscription base fees with usage-based AI compute charges (Hybrid model) -> monthly bills can spike unpredictably if AI usage grows faster than expected.
- **Lowest Paid Entry at $9/mo**: Hugging Face Pro offers the cheapest paid tier among all 10 at **$9/mo**, while Workato's base workspace starts near **$10K/year** -> the spread between cheapest and most expensive entry is over 90x.
- **Fermyon Acquisition Impact**: Fermyon's acquisition by Akamai shifted its enterprise product (Wasm Functions -> Akamai Functions) to a sales-only channel, removing public pricing -> existing Fermyon Cloud tiers remain but enterprise path has changed.
- **Botpress AI Spend Surcharge**: Botpress charges a subscription fee PLUS variable "AI Spend" on every tier, including the free tier ($5 monthly AI credit) -> actual cost depends heavily on conversation volume and model choice.
- **Coval's Tight Tiers**: Coval offers just 3 tiers (Starter $100, Growth $500, Enterprise $4,500) with no free plan, only a 7-day trial -> high entry cost relative to peers may deter small teams.
- **Northflank's Granular Compute**: Northflank bills at **$0.01667/vCPU/hour** with free sandbox covering up to $20/mo of resources -> most transparent infrastructure pricing in this cohort.
- **AWS Bedrock Flex Tier**: A relatively new "Flex" tier offers discounted per-token rates for latency-tolerant workloads -> viable for batch processing but not real-time applications.
- **Altair Free Personal Edition**: Altair AI Studio Personal Edition is free but limited to **10,000 rows** of data with no Auto Model or Turbo Prep -> acts as a trial, not a production tool.
- **Hugging Face Recent Additions**: B200 multi-GPU configurations and ZeroGPU xlarge tier (full H200 access) added in 2025-2026 -> expanding high-end compute options for enterprise inference workloads.", "coval_simulation_and_evaluation_for_ai_agents": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Subscription |
| **free_plan** | false |
| **starts_at_usd** | 100 |
| **mention** | Starts at $100/mo; 7-day free trial |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Starter | 100 | 80 (20% annual discount) | 100 simulation mins/mo, 1,000 monitored calls/mo, 30-day trace retention, unlimited seats, 50 custom metrics, basic voice models | Small teams starting agent evaluation |
| Growth | 500 | 400 (20% annual discount) | 1,000 simulation mins/mo, 10,000 monitored calls/mo, 90-day trace retention, unlimited seats & projects, 250 custom metrics, advanced voice models | Growing voice/chat-agent teams in production |
| Enterprise | 4500 | null | Custom simulation minutes/monitored calls, custom retention, custom voice models, dedicated Slack channel, SAML SSO/SCIM, white-glove onboarding | Regulated industries, high-volume, multi-team orgs |

Coval's pricing page at coval.ai/pricing shows a clean three-tier subscription model. The 20% annual discount is notable - it reduces Starter to **$80/mo** and Growth to **$400/mo** on annual billing. Unlike most AI-platform peers, Coval offers no free plan, only a 7-day trial, which raises the barrier for self-serve adoption. The earlier saasworthy.com listing showing Core at $300/mo and Scale at $999/mo appears to reflect an older pricing structure that has since been revised downward on the official site. Source: [coval_simulation_and_evaluation_for_ai_agents[0]] [67], [coval_simulation_and_evaluation_for_ai_agents[1]] [1]

**recent_changes**: Prior pricing (Core $300/mo, Scale $999/mo per SaaSworthy) appears to have been restructured to Starter $100/mo, Growth $500/mo, Enterprise $4,500/mo on the current site. Exact change date not publicly documented.", "siliconflow_ai_inference_cloud": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Usage-based |
| **free_plan** | true |
| **starts_at_usd** | 0 |
| **mention** | Free tier + pay-as-you-go per token |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Free | 0 | 0 | 14 yuan signup credits, free models (e.g. Qwen2.5-7B forever free), rate-limited access to DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen2.5-72B | Individual developers, experimentation |
| Pay-as-you-go | null (usage-based) | null (usage-based) | Per-token billing for 200+ models, no minimum commitment, OpenAI-compatible API | Production workloads, scaling teams |
| Enterprise | null | null | Contact sales, custom deployments, fine-tuning, SLAs | Enterprise with compliance/SLA needs |

SiliconFlow operates a pure pay-as-you-go model with per-token billing across 200+ models. The free tier provides **14 yuan (~$2 USD) in signup credits** and some smaller models (Qwen2.5-7B) are free indefinitely. The official pricing page (siliconflow.com/pricing) describes "Transparent Pay-as-You-Go" rates with a "Get Started for Free" option and "Contact Sales" for enterprise. Specific per-token prices vary by model; the yangmao.ai source confirms the free tier structure but CloudPrice's API returned no matching records at the time of extraction. Source: [siliconflow_ai_inference_cloud[0]] [7], [siliconflow_ai_inference_cloud[1]] [57]

**recent_changes**: Not publicly documented.", "together_ai_the_ai_native_cloud": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Usage-based |
| **free_plan** | true |
| **starts_at_usd** | 0 |
| **mention** | Start for free, pay per token |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Free | 0 | 0 | $5/mo free credits (non-commercial/educational), serverless inference, community support | Students, researchers, experimentation |
| Serverless Inference | null (usage-based) | null (usage-based) | Per-token pricing across 100+ models, auto-scaling, no minimum commitment | Production inference workloads |
| Dedicated Endpoints | 500+ | null | Guaranteed capacity, 3-month minimum, custom SLAs, 1x H100 at $6.49/hr, HGX B200 at $11+/hr | Teams needing guaranteed throughput |
| Enterprise | null | null | Custom commitments over $10K/mo, dedicated account management, volume discounts | Large-scale enterprise deployments |

Key per-token serverless pricing (per 1M tokens, input/output):
- Llama 3 8B Instruct Lite: **$0.14 / $0.14**
- Qwen3 235B A22B: **$0.20 / $0.60**
- Llama 3.3 70B: **$0.88 / $0.88** (deploybase.ai)
- Kimi K2.6: **$1.20 / $4.50**

Fine-tuning pricing ranges from **$0.48/1M tokens** (SFT LoRA, up to 16B models) to **$25.00/1M tokens** (DeepSeek-V3 Full). GPU clusters: on-demand **$5.49-$9.95/GPU-hr**, reserved **$3.99-$9.65/GPU-hr**. Source: [together_ai_the_ai_native_cloud[0]] [69], [together_ai_the_ai_native_cloud[1]] [14]

**recent_changes**: Not publicly documented. DeployBase article dated September 2025 confirms prices as of March 2026.", "workato_enterprise_integration_and_automation": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Enterprise |
| **free_plan** | false |
| **starts_at_usd** | null |
| **mention** | Contact sales for pricing |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standard | null | null | Unlimited workflows, unlimited connections, SAML SSO/MFA, RBAC, 1 Standard Workbot, 1 concurrency, 30-day log retention, encryption at rest/in transit | Mid-market automation teams |
| Business | null | null | All Standard features + increased concurrency, advanced connectors, premium features | Growing organizations |
| Enterprise | null | null | All Business features + enterprise security, advanced governance, dedicated support, custom integrations | Large enterprises |
| Workato One | null | null | Full platform including Agentic Orchestration | Organizations wanting unified automation + AI agents |

Workato's pricing is entirely quote-based. The official pricing page states "Flexible pricing built for scalability" and directs all prospects to "Schedule a demo" or "Get a quote." According to Lindy.ai, Workato's base workspace typically starts at around **$10,000/year**. SpendHound reports average SMB pricing at **$110,457/year**. G2 lists a starting price of **$75/mo**, though this likely refers to a limited trial or per-seat add-on, not a full workspace. The model is "Platform + Usage": customers pay for one workspace and add recipe packs, with consumption tracked by capability (Event Streams, APIM, Workflows). Source: [workato_enterprise_integration_and_automation[0]] [70], [workato_enterprise_integration_and_automation[1]] [22], [workato_enterprise_integration_and_automation[2]] [21]

**recent_changes**: Not publicly documented.", "northflank_developer_platform_for_scale": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sandbox | 0 | 0 | Always-on compute, 2x free services, 1x free database, 2x free cron jobs, up to $20/mo free resources | Individual developers, prototyping |
| Pay-as-you-go | null (usage-based) | null (usage-based) | Consumption-based billing, infinitely scalable, CPU and GPU deployment, $0.01667/vCPU/hr, multi-region (US/EU/Asia) | Production workloads, scaling teams |
| Enterprise | null | null | VPC runtime, 24/7 support & SLA, FDE onboarding, custom compute plans, enterprise pricing for high-scale deployments | Enterprise with compliance needs |

Northflank offers the most granular and transparent infrastructure pricing in this cohort. The sandbox covers up to **$20/mo** of free resources. Beyond that, compute is billed at **$0.01667/vCPU/hr** with memory at **$0.00556/GB/hr** (based on $4/GB/month). The Nightscout documentation confirms the $20/mo free resource cap. Source: [northflank_developer_platform_for_scale[0]] [18], [northflank_developer_platform_for_scale[1]] [20]

| Enterprise | null (starts ~1,495) | null | Dedicated manager, SLAs, SSO, SOC 2/GDPR compliance, custom message limits, unlimited bots | Enterprise customer support operations |

Botpress uses a hybrid model: each tier charges a subscription fee PLUS variable "AI Spend" for LLM inference costs. The free tier includes **$5 in monthly AI credits** but conversations beyond that incur per-token charges. This means actual monthly costs can significantly exceed the listed subscription price for high-volume deployments. Source: [botpress_ai_agent_platform[0]] [27], [botpress_ai_agent_platform[1]] [29]

**recent_changes**: Introduction of B200 multi-GPU configurations, H100 availability on AWS, ZeroGPU xlarge tier (full H200 access), and over-quota credit pricing, reported May 2026 by Metacto.", "altair_ai_studio_data_science_and_ml_platform": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Freemium |
| **free_plan** | true |
| **starts_at_usd** | null |
| **mention** | Free Personal Edition; contact sales for Standard |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Personal Edition | 0 | 0 | GA Software Access, Data Connectivity, ML Algorithms, Visualizations, Scripting, Extensibility, up to 10,000 rows, no indemnities/support | Individuals, students, light exploration |
| Standard Edition | null | null (annual subscription) | GA Software Access, Data Connectivity, ML Algorithms, Visualizations, Auto Model, Turbo Prep, Interactive Analysis, Radoop Extension, no data limit, indemnities & support | Business teams, production data science |
| Academic Edition | null | null (annual subscription) | All Standard features, licensed for schools/universities/students, Workstation + Server + Cloud architecture | Academic institutions, research |

Altair AI Studio (formerly RapidMiner Studio) was acquired by Siemens. The Personal Edition is free but severely limited: **10,000-row data cap**, no Auto Model, no Turbo Prep, no Interactive Analysis, and no support. Standard and Academic editions require an annual subscription with no public pricing; Capterra lists "Get Price" (contact vendor). The Altair Units licensing system means pricing depends on an organization's existing Altair unit pool. Source: [altair_ai_studio_data_science_and_ml_platform[0]] [68], [altair_ai_studio_data_science_and_ml_platform[1]] [36]

**recent_changes**: Siemens acquisition of Altair completed; no specific pricing change date publicly documented.", "fermyon_serverless_webassembly_cloud": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Freemium |
| **free_plan** | true |
| **starts_at_usd** | 19.38 |
| **mention** | Free + paid plans from $19.38/mo |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Starter | 0 | 0 | Up to 5 apps, 100,000 requests, 1GB KV storage, 5GB SQLite database storage, community Discord support | Individual developers, hobby projects |
| Growth | 19.38 | null | Up to 100 apps, 1,000,000 requests, 2GB KV storage, 50GB SQLite database storage, next-business-day email support | Growing serverless Wasm projects |
| Enterprise | null | null | Custom usage quotas, custom support, custom SLAs, Fermyon Cloud or Akamai Functions integration | Enterprise edge-compute deployments |

Fermyon was acquired by Akamai, and its enterprise product (Fermyon Wasm Functions) has transitioned to **Akamai Functions**, priced exclusively through Akamai sales. The Fermyon Cloud tiers (Starter, Growth, Enterprise) remain publicly listed at fermyon.com/pricing. The Growth tier at **$19.38/mo** is among the lowest paid entries in this cohort. Source: [fermyon_serverless_webassembly_cloud[0]] [45], [fermyon_serverless_webassembly_cloud[1]] [41]

**recent_changes**: Fermyon Wasm Functions transitioned to Akamai Functions, an enterprise edge product priced through Akamai sales engagement without a public rate card (DevTune, 2025).", "aws_bedrock_managed_foundation_model_service": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Usage-based |
| **free_plan** | false |
| **starts_at_usd** | null |
| **mention** | Pay per token; no minimum |

**Tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| On-Demand | null (usage-based) | null (usage-based) | Per-token billing, no minimum commitment, pay for input + output tokens separately, supports all Bedrock models | Variable inference workloads, prototyping |
| Provisioned Throughput | null (reserved monthly) | null (reserved) | Reserved throughput units at fixed monthly fee, guaranteed capacity, 1-6 month commitments, custom model support | Steady-state production workloads needing guaranteed throughput |
| Flex Tier | null (usage-based, discounted) | null (usage-based, discounted) | Discounted per-token rates for latency-tolerant workloads, trades immediate processing for cost efficiency | Batch processing, non-real-time workloads |

Key on-demand per-token pricing (per 1M tokens, approximate ranges):
- Claude 3.5 Haiku: **$0.80 input / $4.00 output**
- Claude 3.5 Sonnet: **$3.00 input / $15.00 output**
- Llama 3.1 8B: **$0.07 input / $0.07 output**
- Llama 3.1 70B: **$0.40 input / $0.40 output**
- Titan Text Premier: **$0.50 input / $1.50 output**

AWS Bedrock has no standalone free tier; pricing is purely per-token. However, AWS Free Tier credits and startup programs can cover Bedrock usage. The Flex tier is a newer option offering discounted rates for workloads tolerant of non-immediate processing. Model pricing ranges from **$0.07 to $75 per 1M tokens** depending on model and tier. Source: [aws_bedrock_managed_foundation_model_service[0]] [66], [aws_bedrock_managed_foundation_model_service[1]] [50], [aws_bedrock_managed_foundation_model_service[2]] [52]

**recent_changes**: Flex tier introduced for discounted latency-tolerant inference; specific date not publicly documented.", "comparative_analysis": "| Company | Model | Free Plan | Lowest Paid ($/mo) | Entry Barrier | Pricing Transparency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Coval | Subscription | No | 100 | Medium | High (prices listed) |
| SiliconFlow | Usage-based | Yes | 0 (PAYG) | Very Low | Medium (per-token varies by model) |
| Together AI | Usage-based | Yes | 0 (PAYG) | Very Low | High (per-token listed) |
| Workato | Enterprise | No | null (contact sales) | Very High | Very Low (no public prices) |
| Northflank | Hybrid | Yes | 0 (PAYG) | Very Low | Very High (vCPU/hr listed) |
| Botpress | Hybrid | Yes | 89 | Medium | Medium (subscription + variable AI spend) |
| Hugging Face | Hybrid | Yes | 9 | Low | High (all tiers + usage pricing) |
| Altair AI Studio | Freemium | Yes | null (contact sales) | High for paid | Very Low (no public prices for paid) |
| Fermyon | Freemium | Yes | 19.38 | Low | High (prices listed) |
| AWS Bedrock | Usage-based | No | null (per-token) | Low-Medium | High (per-token by model) |

The table reveals a clear split: inference/AI platforms (SiliconFlow, Together AI, AWS Bedrock) favor pure usage-based pricing with low or no entry barriers, while enterprise automation/ML platforms (Workato, Altair) hide pricing behind sales walls. Hybrid models (Botpress, Hugging Face, Northflank) blend subscription predictability with variable compute, creating both flexibility and bill-shock risk.", "synthesis": "Three distinct pricing archetypes emerge across these 10 companies:

**Archetype 1 - Pure Usage-Based (SiliconFlow, Together AI, AWS Bedrock)**: These inference providers charge per token with no monthly minimum. The mechanism is simple: lower marginal cost per token drives adoption, and scale economics mean high-volume customers are profitable even at low per-unit prices. The implication for buyers is that monthly costs are unpredictable and depend entirely on workload volume. Recommendation: model worst-case token volumes before committing; use reserved throughput (Together Dedicated Endpoints, AWS Provisioned Throughput) for steady-state workloads to cap costs.

**Archetype 2 - Opaque Enterprise (Workato, Altair AI Studio)**: Both require sales engagement for any pricing, publishing no numbers publicly. Workato's workspace-plus-recipe model and Altair's unit-based licensing create complex, negotiated deals. The mechanism is value-based pricing: the vendor captures more margin by anchoring negotiations to customer-specific ROI rather than a published rate card. The implication is that procurement cycles are long (weeks to months), and price discrimination between customers is significant. Recommendation: use peer benchmarks (SpendHound reports Workato average SMB at ~$110K/yr) and competitive quotes as negotiation leverage.

**Archetype 3 - Hybrid Freemium (Botpress, Hugging Face, Northflank, Fermyon, Coval)**: These platforms offer a free or low-cost entry tier with paid upgrades. The key tension is between the free tier's limits (Botpress's 500 messages, Altair's 10K rows, Fermyon's 5 apps) and production needs. Botpress exemplifies the hidden-cost risk: the "Plus" tier at $89/mo sounds moderate, but AI Spend surcharges can double or triple the effective monthly bill. Hugging Face navigates this best by clearly separating subscription benefits (ZeroGPU quota, storage) from usage costs (Inference Endpoints at $0.033-$80/hr). Recommendation: always calculate the total cost including variable components, not just the subscription line item.

A non-obvious divergence: Northflank and Fermyon, both developer-infrastructure platforms, chose opposite paths for their free tiers. Northflank offers always-on compute in its free Sandbox (up to $20/mo of resources), while Fermyon's Starter allows 5 apps but no always-on compute. This reflects different philosophical bets: Northflank prioritizes "always available" for developer experience, while Fermyon prioritizes resource efficiency for serverless Wasm. For buyers running always-on services, Northflank's free tier is functionally more useful; for event-driven workloads, Fermyon's model aligns better."}

citations.title: Cost and Pricing plans - Coval
excerpts: The pricing for Coval starts at $300.0 per month. Coval has 2 different plans: Core at $300.00 per month. Scale at $999.00 per month.
